findings.title: Absolute Filesystem Path Leaked in Response
findings.description: The index response includes the absolute project root path in JSON response
findings.impact: Low for local IPC (Unix socket 0600 permissions), would be High for network communication
findings.remediation: Remove project_root field from index response JSON

passed_checks.evidence: All SQL queries use parameterized binding via rusqlite params! macro
Symlinks prevented with follow_links(false), hidden files filtered, supported file types only
All parameters validated before use, regex compiled with error handling
Unix sockets created with 0600 permissions, Windows pipes use ACLs, read timeout 5s
Invalid JSON returns E003 error, type-safe parameter extraction
No API keys, passwords, or private keys detected in code
No known CVEs in rusqlite 0.31, serde_json 1.0, regex 1.10, walkdir 2.5, interprocess 2.0
Atomic counters with SeqCst ordering, Mutex-protected storage, RwLock state transitions
Generic error messages (E001, E002, E003), detailed errors logged server-side only

A02_Broken_Authentication.finding: IPC socket authentication via OS-level permissions

A03_Broken_Access_Control.finding: Restrictive socket permissions (0600 Unix, ACLs Windows)

A04_Insecure_Design.finding: Threat model appropriate for local IPC daemon

A05_Security_Misconfiguration.finding: No debug mode enabled, no sensitive configuration exposed

A09_Logging_and_Monitoring_Failures.finding: Detailed server-side logging, generic client responses

A10_SSRF.finding: No external entity processing or network requests

medium.title: Remove absolute path from index response
medium.action: Delete project_root field from JSON response
medium.rationale: Prevents filesystem path disclosure for future network variants

security_test_gaps.description: Verify symlinks outside project root are not indexed
Verify invalid JSON returns proper error response

recommended_fixes_before_network: Add rate limiting on index requests
Implement request logging with correlation IDs

auditor_notes: Proper use of parameterized queries eliminates SQL injection risk
Symlink prevention correctly prevents directory traversal attacks
Socket permissions properly restrict access to owner
Only identified issue is information disclosure in response
Medium severity finding has minimal impact for local IPC use case
Suitable for deployment as-is for local daemon communication
final_status: APPROVED for local IPC daemon deployment with minor recommendation to address information disclosure before network extension
